verbatim: Full pipeline runs on a single H200 (141 GB VRAM). Stages 2.5, 3, 4, and 5 keep the student model resident between stages — no inter-stage reload.
claim_summary: Single H200 with 141 GB VRAM; resident model across stages 2.5-5

verbatim: Stage 1 requires a single forward pass (~5 min on H200); all subsequent GRAPE computation is weight-space only.
claim_summary: Stage 1 ~5 min wall-time on H200

verbatim: H200. Original BF16 model (~70 GB) leaves 71 GB VRAM headroom. Single forward pass over 256 calibration samples (~5 min), then weight-space GRAPE computation on GPU.
claim_summary: Stage 1: BF16 model 70 GB, 71 GB headroom on H200

verbatim: This is a single forward pass (~5 min on H200).
claim_summary: Phase A calibration single forward pass ~5 min on H200

verbatim: H200. Model (70 GB BF16) stays loaded from Stage 1. 71 GB VRAM headroom enables batch_size=6 for profiling.
claim_summary: Stage 2 profiling uses batch_size=6 with 71 GB H200 headroom

verbatim: Estimated wall-clock reduction on the cost-matrix phase: 10–100× (from minutes of Python iteration to seconds of tensor ops). Not yet implemented
claim_summary: Vectorized REAM accumulators projected 10-100x speedup (planned)

verbatim: The early-exit optimization provides the dominant ~2× speedup
claim_summary: Stage 2 early-exit profiling ~2x speedup

verbatim: Stored in `_stage2_input_covariance.pt` (fp16 storage to avoid bf16 precision loss). On H200 with batch_size=6, the covariance accumulates signal across all 1024 calibration samples
claim_summary: Covariance stored as fp16 (avoiding bf16 precision loss)

verbatim: H200 required (teacher BF16 ~70 GB + student BF16 ~50 GB = ~120 GB VRAM)
claim_summary: Stage 2.5 needs ~120 GB VRAM (teacher+student BF16)

verbatim: Teacher precision | BF16 — both models fit on H200 | BF16
claim_summary: Stage 2.5 teacher precision BF16 on H200

verbatim: H200. Pruned student model stays resident from Stage 2.5. Original BF16 model also loaded for cross-covariance dual-forward (~120 GB VRAM total).
claim_summary: Stage 3 dual-model dual-forward at ~120 GB VRAM

verbatim: Both the original (teacher) model and the pruned (student) model are loaded in VRAM simultaneously (~70 GB + ~50 GB = ~120 GB on H200's 141 GB).
claim_summary: Stage 3 Phase A: 70+50=120 GB on H200's 141 GB

verbatim: each candidate requires ~2 minutes for a full 40-layer factor pass and ~20 seconds for PPL evaluation on H200. No model copies are made — originals are snapshotted to CPU RAM (~50 GB; H200 has 256 GB host RAM) and restored after each evaluation. Total α search: ~33 minutes for 11 candidates.
claim_summary: α search ~33 min total; uses 50 GB host RAM on H200's 256 GB

verbatim: If host RAM headroom at α-search entry is insufficient (<15 GB available), Stage 3 raises RuntimeError immediately rather than degrade to a spectral proxy
claim_summary: α-search requires ≥15 GB host RAM free; raises RuntimeError otherwise

verbatim: Path 1 — Paper-exact Theorem 3.2 (primary on H200, cross-covariance C available)
claim_summary: AA-SVD Path 1 (Theorem 3.2) is primary on H200

verbatim: This eliminates N_experts × N_layers redundant eigh(2048×2048) calls (~7,200 for 180 experts × 40 layers). ... Estimated wall-clock reduction: ~25% on Phase C.
claim_summary: Eigh caching skips ~7200 redundant calls; ~25% Phase C speedup

verbatim: Dtype-aware noise floor: bf16→1e-2, fp16→1e-3, fp32→1e-6
claim_summary: Dtype-aware noise floors for SVD eigenvalues

verbatim: H200. Activation covariance reused from Stage 2 — no additional forward passes needed. FactoredExperts model stays resident from Stage 3; _stage3_original_weights.pt remains in CPU RAM. No inter-stage reload.
claim_summary: Stage 4 reuses Stage 2 covariance; originals on CPU; no reload

verbatim: H200. EoRA-compensated student model stays resident from Stage 4. Teacher loads in BF16 (~70 GB); combined VRAM ~126 GB (with bs=8 logits).
claim_summary: Stage 5 ~126 GB combined VRAM at bs=8

verbatim: On H200 with ~16 GB VRAM headroom at bs=8, chunk_size=512 (the full sequence length) is safe — peak intermediate is ~2.4 GB.
claim_summary: Stage 5 chunk_size=512 safe at bs=8 (~2.4 GB peak, 16 GB headroom)

verbatim: When torch_compile: true in the stage config, both teacher and student models are compiled via torch.compile(model, mode="reduce-overhead") before the KD training loop. On H200 (Hopper architecture), this enables kernel fusion and reduced launch overhead. Expected speedup: 20–40% on forward pass throughput after a one-time ~2–5 min compilation cost.
claim_summary: torch.compile reduce-overhead gives 20-40% speedup on H200 Hopper

verbatim: Quality impact: zero — torch.compile produces numerically identical outputs in default mode.
claim_summary: torch.compile claimed numerically identical to eager

verbatim: Learning rate 5×10⁻⁵ ... Batch size 8 ... Gradient accumulation 1 ... Effective batch size 8 ... Max sequence length 512
claim_summary: Stage 5 hyperparams: lr=5e-5, bs=8, grad_accum=1, seq=512

verbatim: Teacher precision BF16 — H200 (141 GB VRAM) fits teacher+student in BF16 with ~15 GB headroom at bs=8. Student is always BF16.
claim_summary: Both teacher and student BF16 on H200 with 15 GB headroom

verbatim: On H200 (141 GB VRAM), both teacher and student load in BF16 (~70 GB + ~50 GB = ~120 GB). At bs=8 with seq_len=512, vocabulary logits consume ~5 GB, leaving ~16 GB headroom.
claim_summary: Stage 5 VRAM accounting: 70+50+5 GB; 16 GB headroom on 141 GB H200

verbatim: Runs on the same H200 instance as Stage 5 (student model stays resident).
claim_summary: Stage 6 reuses H200 instance from Stage 5

verbatim: WikiText-2 PPL | Standard next-token NLL → exp(mean_NLL), seq_len=2048
claim_summary: WikiText-2 PPL eval at seq_len=2048

verbatim: WikiText-2 PPL | #1 — batch_size=1→8 | ppl_batch_size | 8
claim_summary: PPL batch_size raised 1→8

verbatim: ARC-C + HellaSwag | #2 — lm-eval batch_size=1→auto:8 | lm_eval_batch_size | "auto:8"
claim_summary: lm-eval batch_size auto:8 for zero-shot tasks

verbatim: HumanEval (164 prompts) | #3 — batched model.generate() | gen_batch_size | 8 ; MATH-500 (500 prompts) | #4 — batched model.generate() | gen_batch_size | 8
claim_summary: Generative tasks (HumanEval/MATH-500) batched at gen_batch_size=8

verbatim: torch.compile (#5, torch_compile: true): Before any evaluation begins, model.forward is compiled via torch.compile(model.forward, dynamic=True, mode="reduce-overhead"). dynamic=True handles variable-length padded batches from lm-eval. One-time compilation cost (~3–5 min on H200) is amortized across 1000+ forward passes. Only the forward pass is compiled — model.generate() is NOT compiled because autoregressive decoding changes shapes every step
claim_summary: Stage 6 compile forward only (dynamic=True), 3-5 min cost

verbatim: the teacher preload begins during Phase 1's generative evals ... eliminating the ~3–5 min dead time that a blocking teacher load would cause.
claim_summary: Teacher I/O overlap eliminates 3-5 min dead time

verbatim: Subsequent runs: Cached teacher results are loaded directly. No teacher model load, no teacher evaluation. This eliminates ~50% of total Stage 6 wall-clock time.
claim_summary: Teacher eval cache eliminates ~50% Stage 6 time

verbatim: the GGUF conversion (convert_hf_to_gguf.py) runs simultaneously in a background CPU thread ... CPU-only, ~5–10 min
claim_summary: GGUF conversion ~5-10 min, overlapped with teacher eval

verbatim: #1 PPL batch_size=8 — NLL is computed per-token; out.loss × (batch.numel() - batch.shape[0]) recovers the exact sum regardless of batch size
claim_summary: Batched PPL claimed numerically identical via per-token sum

verbatim: #2 lm-eval batch_size=auto:8 — lm-eval's loglikelihood scoring is deterministic and batch-size-independent — left-padding with causal attention mask prevents cross-contamination
claim_summary: lm-eval auto:8 batch claimed numerically identical

verbatim: #3, #4 Batched generate — Greedy decoding (do_sample=False) produces the same argmax at each step regardless of batch. Left-padding with attention_mask ensures each prompt sees only its own context
claim_summary: Batched greedy generate claimed numerically identical via left-pad+mask

verbatim: #5 torch.compile — No numerical approximations in default/reduce-overhead modes.
claim_summary: torch.compile in default/reduce-overhead modes claimed numerically identical

verbatim: Baseline (batch_size=1, no cache) | ~90–150 min | ~90–150 min | ~20 min | ~200–320 min
claim_summary: Stage 6 baseline ~200-320 min

verbatim: Expected improvement: ~8–12× end-to-end wall-clock reduction, from ~3–5 hours down to ~25–30 minutes.
claim_summary: Stage 6 ~8-12x speedup; 3-5 hr → 25-30 min

verbatim: HF Jobs bucket FUSE mounts are not durable under SIGKILL or timeout. The durability boundary is per-stage Hub uploads
claim_summary: HF Jobs bucket FUSE non-durable; Hub commit is the durability boundary

verbatim: if all per-layer B-cov spill files exist in _stage3_bcov_partial/, Phase A (covariance collection) is skipped entirely — including the teacher model load (~70 GB, ~60s). The α search result is cached in _stage3_alpha_result.json and reused on re-entry (~33 min saved).
claim_summary: Stage 3 resume saves teacher load (70 GB / 60s) and α search (33 min)

verbatim: Max calibration samples 3000 ... KD temperature (τ) 1.0
claim_summary: Router KD: 3000 calibration samples, τ=1.0

verbatim: torch.compile | true | H200 Hopper. 20–40% forward pass speedup. Set false for debugging or hardware without compile support.
claim_summary: torch.compile default true on H200 Hopper, 20-40% speedup
